requirements.text: Environment Builder MUST be implemented using Nix or equivalent hermetic stable-digest build system.

Lockfile MUST pin the minimum artifact set. Model files are pinned per-file by SHA256 digest via resolver/HF resolution (implemented). The software stack (serving stack, CUDA/container, kernel libraries, network stack) is recorded via the Nix runtime closure (runtime_closure_digest / build.nix_closure); a real content-addressed closure is pinned only when the builder runs against an actual Nix closure (--nix-store-path / --closure-digest, as the deployment path does). The default builder path records closure metadata only.
Lockfile MUST include digest and retrieval info for every artifact.
Lockfile MUST record runtime_closure_digest.

Compiled extension outputs are pinned within the hermetic Nix runtime closure (runtime_closure_digest / build.nix_closure). The builder currently records closure metadata only (scaffolding); real per-artifact pinning requires building against an actual Nix closure. Consistent with SPEC-6.1-NIX-CLOSURE-CONTENT.

Networking stack binaries and PMD/driver artifacts would be pinned within the Nix runtime closure; no deterministic networking stack is implemented yet (frames are synthetic), so this is not enforced. See SPEC-9.1-NETSTACK-CLOSURE-PIN.
External artifacts MUST be content-addressed and digest-verified before use.
Runner MUST refuse to execute when digests mismatch.
trust_remote_code SHOULD default to false.
If remote code is enabled it MUST be pinned by commit and hashed.
Resolver MUST resolve HF refs to immutable commit SHA.
Resolver MUST enumerate required HF files (weights, config, tokenizer, generation config, chat template, prompt formatting logic).
Resolver MUST compute and record per-file digest and retrieval metadata.
Same manifest MUST resolve to same lockfile bytes.
Runner MUST fetch artifacts only from lockfile entries and verify digests.
Large deployments SHOULD use an internal digest-keyed HF mirror.
Nix closure MUST include vLLM, torch, CUDA userspace/container equivalent, kernel libs, net stack, PMD artifacts.
Kubernetes workloads MUST reference images by immutable digest.
Each run MUST include exact manifest, exact lockfile, and runtime_closure_digest.
Inputs MUST be mounted into pod and recorded in run bundle.
If strict_hardware=true runner MUST refuse non-conforming hardware.
If strict_hardware=false runner MUST label result non-conformant and report diffs.
Kubernetes scheduling SHOULD enforce constraints via labels/affinity/taints.
Runner MUST validate runtime conformance and enforce strictness.
Manifest MUST specify engine trace enablement and event set.
Runner MUST include engine trace in run bundle when enabled.
System MUST route network I/O through deterministic userspace stack.
Full networking stack closure MUST be pinned and recorded.
Egress traffic MUST be reproducible at L2 per manifest scope and ordering rules.
Manifest MUST define and pin MTU/MSS/segmentation/offloads/threading/queue/ring/internal batching.
Capture MUST not perturb packetization or ordering.
Manifest MUST declare security mode.
Collective stack versions/config MUST be pinned for TP/PP.
Engine trace MUST record collective algorithm selection and backend decisions.
Manifest MUST define comparator semantics per observable (exact/ulp/absrel/hash).
Run bundle MUST include manifest+lockfile, runtime closure, artifact digests, environment, execution trace metadata, network provenance.
Provenance MUST be sufficient for third-party re-run and verification.
Verifier MUST emit verify_report.json and verify_summary.txt.
On mismatch verifier MUST report first divergence, numeric diff stats, batch trace diffs, network trace diffs, environment diffs.
Verifier MUST assign determinism grading classes.
Builder MUST emit stable runtime_closure_digest for identical inputs.
Single-node baseline runner MUST execute deterministically under pinned inputs.
Replicated serving MUST use deterministic dispatcher and reproducible routing.
TP/PP mode MUST record collective algorithm selection in engine traces.
